title: Environment professionals
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate entertainment and recreational activities and operations of a recreational facility, including cruise ships and parks. All personal service managers not listed separately. Manage or coordinate fitness and wellness programs and services. Manage and train staff of wellness specialists, health educators, or fitness instructors. (Based on 22 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Clean, maintain, and prepare supplies and work areas for environmental projects.
Collect samples of gases, soils, water, industrial wastewater, or asbestos products to conduct tests on pollutant levels or identify sources of pollution.

Conduct research, or assist in the conduct of research, including the collection of information and samples, such as blood, water, soil, plants, and animals; analyze experimental data and interpret results to write reports and summaries of findings.

Use computers, computer-interfaced equipment, and robotics to perform work duties; input data into databases; and maintain files, such as hazardous waste databases, chemical usage data, or diagrams showing equipment locations.

Isolate, identify, and prepare specimens for examination; examine and analyze material for presence and concentration of contaminants; weigh, analyze, or measure collected sample particles, such as lead or coal dust to determine concentration of pollutants.
Monitor laboratory work to ensure compliance with set standards; keep detailed logs of all work-related activities; record test data and prepare reports that interpret test results.

Develop or implement programs for monitoring of environmental pollution or radiation, and advise industries or government agencies about environmental policies and standards; discuss test results and analyses with customers.
Inspect workplaces to ensure the absence of health and safety hazards and make recommendations to control or eliminate unsafe conditions at workplaces or public facilities.
Prepare samples or photomicrographs for testing and analysis; calibrate microscopes or test instruments; monitor emission control devices to ensure they comply with regulations.
Monitor and observe experiments, recording production and test data for evaluation by research personnel; provide technical support and services for scientists and engineers.
Conduct feasibility and cost-benefit studies for environmental remediation projects; analyze and distill climate-related research findings to inform stakeholders.
Investigate hazardous conditions, spills, or outbreaks of disease; collect samples for analysis and prepare, maintain, or revise quality assurance documentation.
Provide technical direction on environmental planning and assist in budget implementation and forecasts; manage field offices or involve staff in cooperative ventures.
Develop, implement, or manage plans or programs related to conservation or management of natural resources, and review existing environmental remediation designs.
Create complex and dynamic mathematical models of ecological systems; analyze changes to improve environmental performance and avoid unintended negative consequences.
Conduct environmental impact studies; assess the existing or potential environmental impact of land use projects; and carry out environmental assessments in accordance with applicable standards.
Prepare grant applications to obtain funding for climate change programs or environmental management, and write grants to obtain funding for restoration projects.
Coordinate or manage environmental protection programs or projects, assigning or evaluating work.
Review proposed wetland restoration easements or provide technical recommendations; develop, conduct, or participate in surveys or studies of land uses for corrective action plans.
Develop environmental restoration project schedules and budgets; notify regulatory agencies of deviations from implemented remediation plans.
Respond to complaints or questions on wetland jurisdiction, and provide information or clarification.
Develop site-specific health and safety protocols, such as spill contingency plans; and design, or supervise the design of, systems for control of water, air, or soil quality.
Provide assistance with planning, quality assurance, safety inspection protocols, and sampling as part of a team conducting multimedia inspections at complex facilities.
Review grant applications or make funding recommendations; and compile or interpret biodata to aid in program formulation.
Identify or develop strategies to minimize environmental impact of industrial production processes; and recommend methods to protect the environment from industrial production practices.
Develop or present environmental compliance training or orientation sessions to promote awareness.
Conduct fact-finding or mediation sessions among governmental units or other agencies to resolve disputes, and revisit land users to view implemented practices.
Collaborate with environmental scientists, planners, and specialists to address environmental problems; and inform employees or other parties of environmental issues.
Create habitat management or restoration plans; such as native tree restoration and weed control.
Investigation or surveillance of environmental accidents to ascertain ecological impact; research policies for climate or environmental management.

Develop methods to minimize the impact of production processes based on the study of industrial practices and legislation; and investigate the adaptability of various species to environmental conditions.
Train staff on park programs and conduct training sessions for wellness and fitness staff.
Provide care for park program animals.

Gather information from geographic information systems (GIS) databases or applications to formulate land use recommendations, and create environmental models or simulations using GIS data and knowledge of particular ecosystems.

Apply principles of specialized fields of science, such as agronomy, soil science, forestry, or agriculture, to achieve conservation objectives and implement soil or water management techniques, such as nutrient management and erosion control.
Develop or maintain working relationships with local government staff or board members, and coordinate with federal land managers and other agencies to manage and protect rangelands.
Visit areas affected by erosion problems to identify causes or determine solutions, and monitor environmental impacts of development activities.

Apply new or existing research about natural ecosystems to understand economic and industrial systems in the context of the environment, and evaluate the effectiveness of industrial ecology programs using statistical analysis and applications.
Conduct environmental sustainability assessments using material flow analysis (MFA) or substance flow analysis (SFA), and identify environmental impacts caused by products, systems, or projects.
Write reports or academic papers to communicate findings of climate-related studies and prepare technical and research reports, such as environmental impact reports.

Examine local, regional, or global use and flow of materials or energy in industrial production processes and examine societal issues and their relationship with technical systems and the environment.

Identify or compare the component parts or relationships between the parts of industrial, social, and natural systems, and identify sustainable alternatives to industrial or waste-management practices.
Perform analyses to determine how human behavior can affect, and be affected by, changes in the environment, and conduct environmental audits or inspections of violations.
Prepare plans to manage renewable resources and manage forage resources through fire, herbicide use, or revegetation to maintain a sustainable yield from the land.

Provide scientific or technical guidance, support, coordination, or oversight to governmental agencies, environmental programs, industry, or the public, and process and review environmental permits, licenses, or related materials.
Collect, synthesize, analyze, manage, and report environmental data, including pollution emission measurements and atmospheric monitoring measurements.
Plan and organize public events at the park, and conduct field trips to point out scientific, historic, and natural features of parks or other attractions.
Research stories regarding the area's natural history or environment and prepare brochures and write newspaper articles to promote park programs.
Monitor effects of pollution or land degradation and recommend means of prevention or control.
Provide visitor services, including explaining regulations, answering visitor requests, and providing information about the park and surrounding areas.
Assist with operations of general facilities, such as visitor centers, and perform routine maintenance on park structures.
Develop environmental educational programs and curricula for schools and promote initiatives to mitigate climate change with government or environmental groups.
Supervise fitness or wellness specialists and oversee fitness or recreation facilities, ensuring safe and clean facilities and equipment.
Research environmental effects of land and water use to improve environmental conditions or increase outputs, like crop yields.
Plan and direct construction and maintenance of range improvements, such as fencing, and provide advice on water management and forage production methods.
Analyze data to determine validity, quality, and scientific significance and to interpret correlations between human activities and environmental effects.
Track attendance, participation, or performance data related to wellness events and evaluate fitness and wellness programs to determine their effectiveness.
Teach fitness classes to improve strength, flexibility, conditioning, or general fitness.
Organize and oversee events such as organized runs or walks and plan, organize, or lead group activities for customers.
Administer first aid in emergency situations as needed.
Respond to customer, public, or media requests for information about wellness programs or services.
Conduct needs assessments or surveys to determine interest in, or satisfaction with, wellness and fitness programs or services.
Develop marketing campaigns to promote healthy lifestyles or participation in wellness programs.
Maintain wellness- and fitness-related schedules, records, or reports.
Organize and oversee health screenings or other preventive measures, such as blood pressure screenings.
Identify and apply for project funding to support initiatives.
Evaluate new software publishing systems relating to regulatory compliance and environmental monitoring programs.
Inspect equipment, such as rides and vehicles, for safety and compliance.
Monitor regulatory affairs activities to ensure alignment with corporate sustainability or green initiatives and evaluate regulatory affairs aspects that are environmentally focused.
Remove and did not tag
Develop or coordinate fitness and wellness programs or services, and recommend or approve new program or service offerings.

Manage and monitor compliance with regulatory policies and procedures related to environmental, safety, and health standards; inform management and employees about compliance reporting systems, and conduct internal reviews or audits to ensure compliance procedures are followed.

Develop and implement plans for environmental remediation, including surface or ground water remediation, hazardous waste disposal, and contamination assessments for brownfield sites; conduct environmental restoration studies and prepare documentation for environmental projects.

Train and supervise staff in compliance with health, safety, and environmental regulations, including technical issues and safety practices relevant to operational procedures or fitness and wellness programs.

Establish goals and objectives for environmental projects, including wind farm operations and brownfield redevelopment, and manage site assessments or environmental studies to ensure adherence to operational standards.
Develop relationships and communicate with stakeholders, including customers, local authorities, and environmental agencies to coordinate activities and gather feedback for continuous improvement.
Provide guidance on regulatory compliance and develop strategies for regulatory submissions, including the drafting of reports and applications for permits related to environmental projects.
Monitor and maintain records of environmental and operational performance, ensuring compliance with operational procedures and reporting any discrepancies as needed.
Confer with project personnel and communicate with key stakeholders to identify and resolve problems while determining project requirements and objectives.
